NAME

client.c - 👥 Per-client lifecycle manager: threading coordination, state management, and client lifecycle orchestration

struct client_dispatch_entry_t

#define CLIENT_DISPATCH_HANDLER_COUNT 13
#define CLIENT_DISPATCH_HASH(type)
#define MAX_AUDIO_BATCH 8

Variables


client_manager_t g_client_manager
Global client manager singleton - central coordination point. rwlock_t g_client_manager_rwlock = {0}
Reader-writer lock protecting the global client manager.

Detailed Description

👥 Per-client lifecycle manager: threading coordination, state management, and client lifecycle orchestration

1. Client connection establishment and initializationinitializationPer-client thread creation and management (receive, send, render))Client state management with thread-safe access patternspatternsClient disconnection handling and resource cleanupcleanupHash table management for O(1) client lookupslookupsIntegration point between main.c and other modules THREADING ARCHITECTURE PER CLIENT:

Each connected client spawns multiple dedicated threads:

  • Receive Thread: Handles incoming packets from client (calls protocol.c functions)
  • Send Thread: Manages outgoing packet delivery using packet queues
  • Video Render Thread: Generates ASCII frames at 60fps (render.c)
  • Audio Render Thread: Mixes audio streams at 172fps (render.c)

This per-client threading model provides several advantages:

  • Linear performance scaling (no shared bottlenecks)
  • Fault isolation (one client's issues don't affect others)
  • Simplified synchronization (each client owns its resources)
  • Real-time performance guarantees per client

DATA STRUCTURES AND SYNCHRONIZATION:

The module manages two primary data structures:

1.
client_manager_t (global singleton):
•
Array of client_info_t structs (backing storage)
•
Hash table for O(1) client_id -> client_info_t lookups
•
Protected by reader-writer lock (g_client_manager_rwlock)
•
Allows concurrent reads, exclusive writes
2.
client_info_t (per-client state):
•
Network connection details and capabilities
•
Thread handles and synchronization primitives
•
Media buffers (video/audio) and packet queues
•
Terminal capabilities and rendering preferences
•
Protected by per-client mutex (client_state_mutex)

Synchronization Patterns:

LOCK ORDERING PROTOCOL (prevents deadlocks):

1.
Always acquire g_client_manager_rwlock FIRST
2.
Then acquire per-client mutexes if needed
3.
Release in reverse order

SNAPSHOT PATTERN (reduces lock contention):

1.
Acquire mutex
2.
Copy needed state variables to local copies
3.
Release mutex immediately
4.
Process using local copies without locks

INTEGRATION WITH OTHER MODULES:

  • main.c: Calls add_client() and remove_client() from main loop
  • protocol.c: Functions called by client receive threads for packet processing
  • render.c: Render thread functions created per client
  • stream.c: Stream generation functions called by render threads
  • stats.c: Accesses client data for performance monitoring

THREAD LIFECYCLE MANAGEMENT:

Thread creation order (in add_client()):

1.
Initialize client data structures and mutexes
2.
Create send thread (for outgoing packet delivery)
3.
Create receive thread (for incoming packet processing)
4.
Create render threads (video + audio generation)

Thread termination order (in remove_client()):

1.
Set shutdown flags (causes threads to exit main loops)
2.
Join send thread (cleanest exit, no blocking I/O)
3.
Join receive thread (may be blocked on network I/O)
4.
Join render threads (computational work, clean exit)
5.
Clean up resources (queues, buffers, mutexes)

WHY THIS MODULAR DESIGN:

The original server.c contained all client management code inline, making it:

  • Hard to understand the client lifecycle
  • Difficult to modify threading behavior
  • Impossible to isolate client-related bugs
  • Challenging to add new client features

This modular approach provides:

  • Clear separation of client vs. server concerns
  • Easier testing of client management logic
  • Better code reuse and maintenance
  • Future extensibility for new client types

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

September 2025

Version

2.0 (Post-Modularization)

See also

main.c For overall server architecture

render.c For per-client rendering implementation

protocol.c For client packet processing

613                                                                                                           {
614   log_info("[TCP_ADD_CLIENT] ENTER: client_ip=%s, port=%d", client_ip, port);
615   // Find empty slot WITHOUT holding the global lock
616   // We'll re-verify under lock after allocations complete
617   int slot = -1;
618   int existing_count = 0;
619   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
620     if (slot == -1 && g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] == '\0') {
621       slot = i; // Take first available slot
622     }
623     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0' && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
624       existing_count++;
625     }
626   }
627 
628   // Quick pre-check before expensive allocations
629   log_info("[TCP_DBG] SLOT_CHECK: existing_count=%d, max_clients=%d, slot=%d", existing_count, GET_OPTION(max_clients),
630            slot);
631   if (existing_count >= GET_OPTION(max_clients) || slot == -1) {
632     const char *reject_msg = "SERVER_FULL: Maximum client limit reached\n";
633     ssize_t send_result = socket_send(socket, reject_msg, strlen(reject_msg), 0);
634     if (send_result < 0) {
635       log_warn("Failed to send rejection message to client: %s", SAFE_STRERROR(errno));
636     }
637     return NULL;
638   }
639 
640   // NOW acquire the lock early for generating unique client name
641   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRE_START");
642   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
643   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRED");
644 
645   // Generate unique client ID (noun only, without counter or port)
646   char new_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
647   log_info("[TCP_DBG] GENERATE_ID_START");
648   if (generate_client_id(new_client_id, sizeof(new_client_id)) != 0) {
649     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
650     log_error("Failed to generate unique client ID");
651     return NULL;
652   }
653   log_info("[TCP_DBG] GENERATE_ID_DONE: id=%s", new_client_id);
654 
655   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_RELEASE_START");
656   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
657   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_RELEASED");
658 
659   // DO EXPENSIVE ALLOCATIONS OUTSIDE THE LOCK
660   // This prevents blocking frame processing during client initialization
661   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_CREATE_START: About to create video frame buffer for client_id=%s", new_client_id);
662   video_frame_buffer_t *incoming_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
663   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_CREATE_DONE: Video buffer creation returned %p", (void *)incoming_video_buffer);
664   if (!incoming_video_buffer) {
665     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create video buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
666     log_error("Failed to create video buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
667     return NULL;
668   }
669 
670   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_DONE: About to create audio buffer");
671   audio_ring_buffer_t *incoming_audio_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
672   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_DONE: Audio buffer created");
673   if (!incoming_audio_buffer) {
674     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create audio buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
675     log_error("Failed to create audio buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
676     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
677     return NULL;
678   }
679 
680   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_QUEUE_START: Creating audio queue");
681   packet_queue_t *audio_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(500, 1000, false);
682   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_QUEUE_DONE");
683   if (!audio_queue) {
684     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create audio queue for client");
685     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
686     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
687     return NULL;
688   }
689 
690   log_info("[TCP_DBG] OUTGOING_VID_START: Creating outgoing video buffer");
691   video_frame_buffer_t *outgoing_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
692   log_info("[TCP_DBG] OUTGOING_VID_DONE");
693   if (!outgoing_video_buffer) {
694     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create outgoing video buffer for client");
695     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
696     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
697     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
698     return NULL;
699   }
700 
701   void *send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE, 64, void *);
702   if (!send_buffer) {
703     log_error("Failed to allocate send buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
704     video_frame_buffer_destroy(outgoing_video_buffer);
705     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
706     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
707     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
708     return NULL;
709   }
710 
711   // NOW acquire the lock for the critical section: slot assignment + registration
712   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_START: About to acquire client manager write lock");
713   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
714   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRED: Client manager lock acquired");
715 
716   // Re-check slot availability under lock (another thread might have taken it)
717   if (g_client_manager.clients[slot].client_id[0] != '\0') {
718     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
719     SAFE_FREE(send_buffer);
720     video_frame_buffer_destroy(outgoing_video_buffer);
721     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
722     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
723     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
724 
725     const char *reject_msg = "SERVER_FULL: Slot reassigned, try again\n";
726     socket_send(socket, reject_msg, strlen(reject_msg), 0);
727     return NULL;
728   }
729 
730   // Now we have exclusive access to the slot - do the actual registration
731   client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[slot];
732   memset(client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
733 
734   // Initialize codec capabilities to zero (no codecs supported until CLIENT_CAPABILITIES received)
735   client->codec_capabilities_video = 0;
736   client->codec_capabilities_audio = 0;
737 
738   client->socket = socket;
739   client->is_tcp_client = true;
740   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_id, new_client_id, sizeof(client->client_id) - 1);
741   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_ip, client_ip, sizeof(client->client_ip) - 1);
742   client->port = port;
743   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, true);
744   client->server_ctx = server_ctx; // Store server context for cleanup
745   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, false);
746   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources, 0);
747   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, 0);
748   client->connected_at = time(NULL);
749 
750   memset(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
751   client->crypto_initialized = false;
752 
753   client->pending_packet_type = 0;
754   client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
755   client->pending_packet_length = 0;
756 
757   // Assign pre-allocated buffers
758   client->incoming_video_buffer = incoming_video_buffer;
759   client->incoming_audio_buffer = incoming_audio_buffer;
760   client->audio_queue = audio_queue;
761   client->outgoing_video_buffer = outgoing_video_buffer;
762   client->send_buffer = send_buffer;
763   client->send_buffer_size = MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE;
764 
765   // Generate unique noun-based client name with transport type and port
766   // Note: We're holding the write lock here, so it's safe to iterate existing clients
767   if (generate_client_name(client->display_name, sizeof(client->display_name), g_client_manager.clients_by_id, port,
768                            true /* is_tcp */) != 0) {
769     // Fallback to numeric name if generation fails
770     safe_snprintf(client->display_name, sizeof(client->display_name), "client_%u (tcp:%d)", new_client_id, port);
771   }
772 
773   // Register client with named debug system using the generated name
774   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_CLIENT(client, client->display_name, NULL);
775 
776   // Register all atomic fields for debug tracking
777   register_client_info_atomics(client);
778 
779   log_info("Added new client %s from %s:%d (socket=%d, slot=%d)", new_client_id, client_ip, port, socket, slot);
780   log_debug("Client slot assigned: client_id=%s assigned to slot %d, socket=%d", new_client_id, slot, socket);
781 
782   // Register socket with tcp_server
783   asciichat_error_t reg_result = tcp_server_add_client(server_ctx->tcp_server, socket, client);
784   if (reg_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
785     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to register client socket with tcp_server");
786     log_error("Failed to register client %s socket with tcp_server", new_client_id);
787     // Don't unlock here - error_cleanup will do it
788     goto error_cleanup;
789   }
790 
791   g_client_manager.client_count++;
792   log_debug("Client count updated: now %d clients (added client_id=%s to slot %d)", g_client_manager.client_count,
793             new_client_id, slot);
794 
795   HASH_ADD_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, client);
796   log_debug("Added client %s to uthash table", new_client_id);
797 
798   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
799 
800   // Configure socket OUTSIDE lock
801   configure_client_socket(socket, new_client_id);
802 
803   // Initialize mutexes OUTSIDE lock
804   if (mutex_init(&client->client_state_mutex, "client_state") != 0) {
805     log_error("Failed to initialize client state mutex for client %s", new_client_id);
806     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
807     return NULL;
808   }
809 
810   if (mutex_init(&client->send_mutex, "client_send") != 0) {
811     log_error("Failed to initialize send mutex for client %s", new_client_id);
812     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
813     return NULL;
814   }
815 
816   // Register audio and video buffer atomic fields for debug tracking
817   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
818     audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics(client->incoming_audio_buffer, new_client_id);
819   }
820 
821   // Register with audio mixer OUTSIDE lock
822   if (g_audio_mixer && client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
823     if (mixer_add_source(g_audio_mixer, new_client_id, client->incoming_audio_buffer) < 0) {
824       log_warn("Failed to add client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
825     } else {
826 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
827       log_debug("Added client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
828 #endif
829     }
830   }
831 
832   // Perform crypto handshake before starting threads.
833   // This ensures the handshake uses the socket directly without interference from receive thread.
834   if (server_crypto_init() == 0) {
835     // Set timeout for crypto handshake to prevent indefinite blocking
836     // This prevents clients from connecting but never completing the handshake
837     const uint64_t HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_NS = 30ULL * NS_PER_SEC_INT;
838     asciichat_error_t timeout_result = set_socket_timeout(socket, HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_NS);
839     if (timeout_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
840       log_warn("Failed to set handshake timeout for client %s: %s", new_client_id,
841                asciichat_error_string(timeout_result));
842       // Continue anyway - timeout is a safety feature, not critical
843     }
844 
845     int crypto_result = server_crypto_handshake(client);
846     if (crypto_result != 0) {
847       log_error("Crypto handshake failed for client %s: %s", new_client_id, network_error_string());
848       if (remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id) != 0) {
849         log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
850       }
851       return NULL;
852     }
853 
854     // Clear socket timeout after handshake completes successfully
855     // This allows normal operation without timeouts on data transfer
856     asciichat_error_t clear_timeout_result = set_socket_timeout(socket, 0);
857     if (clear_timeout_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
858       log_warn("Failed to clear handshake timeout for client %s: %s", new_client_id,
859                asciichat_error_string(clear_timeout_result));
860       // Continue anyway - we can still communicate even with timeout set
861     }
862 
863     log_debug("Crypto handshake completed successfully for client %s", new_client_id);
864 
865     // Create ACIP transport for protocol-agnostic packet sending
866     // The transport wraps the socket with encryption context from the handshake
867     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_server_get_context(new_client_id);
868     client->transport = acip_tcp_transport_create(new_client_id, socket, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
869     if (!client->transport) {
870       log_error("Failed to create ACIP transport for client %s", new_client_id);
871       if (remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id) != 0) {
872         log_error("Failed to remove client after transport creation failure");
873       }
874       return NULL;
875     }
876     log_debug("Created ACIP transport for client %s with crypto context", new_client_id);
877 
878     // After handshake completes, the client immediately sends PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES
879     // We must read and process this packet BEFORE starting the receive thread to avoid a race condition
880     // where the packet arrives but no thread is listening for it.
881     //
882     // SPECIAL CASE: If client used --no-encrypt, we already received this packet during the handshake
883     // attempt and stored it in pending_packet_*. Use that instead of receiving a new one.
884     packet_envelope_t envelope;
885     bool used_pending_packet = false;
886 
887     if (client->pending_packet_payload) {
888       // Client used --no-encrypt mode - use the packet we already received
889       log_info("Client %s using --no-encrypt mode - processing pending packet type %u", client->client_id,
890                client->pending_packet_type);
891       envelope.type = client->pending_packet_type;
892       envelope.data = client->pending_packet_payload;
893       envelope.len = client->pending_packet_length;
894       envelope.allocated_buffer = client->pending_packet_payload; // Will be freed below
895       envelope.allocated_size = client->pending_packet_length;
896       used_pending_packet = true;
897 
898       // Clear pending packet fields
899       client->pending_packet_type = 0;
900       client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
901       client->pending_packet_length = 0;
902     } else {
903       // Normal encrypted mode - receive capabilities packet
904       log_debug("Waiting for initial capabilities packet from client %s", client->client_id);
905 
906       // Protect crypto context access with client state mutex
907       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
908       const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_server_get_context(client->client_id);
909 
910       // Use per-client crypto state to determine enforcement
911       // At this point, handshake is complete, so crypto_initialized=true and handshake is ready
912       bool enforce_encryption = !GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && client->crypto_initialized &&
913                                 crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
914 
915       packet_recv_result_t result = receive_packet_secure(socket, (void *)crypto_ctx, enforce_encryption, &envelope);
916       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
917 
918       if (result != PACKET_RECV_SUCCESS) {
919         log_error("Failed to receive initial capabilities packet from client %s: result=%d", client->client_id, result);
920         if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
921           buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
922         }
923         if (remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id) != 0) {
924           log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
925         }
926         return NULL;
927       }
928     }
929 
930     if (envelope.type != PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES) {
931       log_error("Expected PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES but got packet type %d from client %s", envelope.type,
932                 client->client_id);
933       if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
934         buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
935       }
936       if (remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id) != 0) {
937         log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
938       }
939       return NULL;
940     }
941 
942     // Process the capabilities packet directly
943     log_debug("Processing initial capabilities packet from client %s (from %s)", client->client_id,
944               used_pending_packet ? "pending packet" : "network");
945     handle_client_capabilities_packet(client, envelope.data, envelope.len);
946 
947     // Free the packet data
948     if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
949       buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
950     }
951     log_debug("Successfully received and processed initial capabilities for client %s", client->client_id);
952   }
953 
954   // Start all client threads in the correct order (unified path for TCP and WebRTC)
955   // This creates: receive thread -> render threads -> send thread
956   // The render threads MUST be created before send thread to avoid the race condition
957   // where send thread reads empty frames before render thread generates the first real frame
958   const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;
959   if (start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, true) != 0) {
960     log_error("Failed to start threads for TCP client %s", client_id_snapshot);
961     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
962     remove_client(server_ctx, client_id_snapshot);
963     return NULL;
964   }
965   log_debug("Successfully created render threads for client %s", client_id_snapshot);
966 
967   // Register client with session_host (for discovery mode support)
968   if (server_ctx->session_host) {
969     client->session_client_id = session_host_add_client(server_ctx->session_host, socket, client_ip, port);
970     if (client->session_client_id == 0) {
971       log_warn("Failed to register client %s with session_host", client_id_snapshot);
972     } else {
973       log_debug("Client %s registered with session_host as %u", client_id_snapshot, client->session_client_id);
974     }
975   }
976 
977   // Broadcast server state to ALL clients AFTER the new client is fully set up
978   // This notifies all clients (including the new one) about the updated grid
979   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
980 
981   return client;
982 
983 error_cleanup:
984   // Clean up all partially allocated resources
985   // NOTE: This label is reached when allocation or initialization fails BEFORE
986   // the client is added to the hash table. Don't call remove_client() here.
987   cleanup_client_all_buffers(client);
988   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
989   return NULL;
990 }






References acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    atomic_load_bool(), atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_u64(), audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture(),
    audio_ring_buffer_destroy(),
    audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics(),
    broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(), buffer_pool_free(),
    crypto_handshake_is_ready(), crypto_server_get_context(),
    g_audio_mixer, g_client_manager,
    g_client_manager_rwlock, generate_client_id(),
    generate_client_name(), handle_client_capabilities_packet(),
    mixer_add_source(), mutex_init(),

client_info_t
  * add_webrtc_client (server_context_t * server_ctx,
  acip_transport_t * transport, const char * client_ip, bool
  start_threads)


Register a WebRTC client with the server. Registers a client that
    connected via WebRTC data channel instead of TCP socket. This function
    reuses most of add_client() logic but skips:


    
  
  • Crypto handshake (already done via ACDS signaling)
    • 
  
  • Socket-specific configuration
    • 
  
  • TCP thread pool registration
    • 



DIFFERENCES FROM add_client():


    
  
  • Takes an already-created acip_transport_t* instead of socket
    • 
  
  • No crypto handshake (WebRTC signaling handled authentication)
    • 
  
  • No socket configuration (WebRTC handles buffering)
    • 
  
  • Uses generic thread spawning instead of tcp_server thread pool
    • 



Parameters


server_ctx Server context


transport WebRTC transport (already created and connected)


client_ip Client IP address for logging (may be empty for P2P)


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t on success, NULL on
  failure


Note


The transport must be fully initialized and ready to
  send/receive



Client capabilities are still expected as first packet




Definition at line 1015 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1016                                                      {
1017   if (!server_ctx || !transport || !client_ip) {
1018     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters to add_webrtc_client");
1019     return NULL;
1020   }
1021 
1022   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1023 
1024   // Find empty slot - this is the authoritative check
1025   int slot = -1;
1026   int existing_count = 0;
1027   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1028     if (slot == -1 && g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] == '\0') {
1029       slot = i; // Take first available slot
1030     }
1031     // Count only active clients
1032     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0' && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
1033       existing_count++;
1034     }
1035   }
1036 
1037   // Check if we've hit the configured max-clients limit (not the array size)
1038   if (existing_count >= GET_OPTION(max_clients)) {
1039     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1040     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, "Maximum client limit reached (%d/%d active clients)", existing_count,
1041               GET_OPTION(max_clients));
1042     log_error("Maximum client limit reached (%d/%d active clients)", existing_count, GET_OPTION(max_clients));
1043     return NULL;
1044   }
1045 
1046   if (slot == -1) {
1047     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1048     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, "No available client slots (all %d array slots are in use)", MAX_CLIENTS);
1049     log_error("No available client slots (all %d array slots are in use)", MAX_CLIENTS);
1050     return NULL;
1051   }
1052 
1053   // Update client_count to match actual count before adding new client
1054   g_client_manager.client_count = existing_count;
1055 
1056   // Generate unique client ID for WebRTC client (noun only, without counter or port)
1057   char new_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
1058   if (generate_client_id(new_client_id, sizeof(new_client_id)) != 0) {
1059     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1060     log_error("Failed to generate unique client ID for WebRTC client");
1061     return NULL;
1062   }
1063 
1064   // Initialize client
1065   client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[slot];
1066 
1067   // Free any existing buffers from previous client in this slot
1068   if (client->incoming_video_buffer) {
1069     video_frame_buffer_destroy(client->incoming_video_buffer);
1070     client->incoming_video_buffer = NULL;
1071   }
1072   if (client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
1073     video_frame_buffer_destroy(client->outgoing_video_buffer);
1074     client->outgoing_video_buffer = NULL;
1075   }
1076   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1077     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(client->incoming_audio_buffer);
1078     client->incoming_audio_buffer = NULL;
1079   }
1080   if (client->send_buffer) {
1081     SAFE_FREE(client->send_buffer);
1082     client->send_buffer = NULL;
1083   }
1084 
1085   memset(client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
1086 
1087   // Set up WebRTC-specific fields
1088   client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE; // WebRTC has no traditional socket
1089   client->is_tcp_client = false;         // WebRTC client - threads managed directly
1090   client->transport = transport;         // Use provided transport
1091   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_id, new_client_id, sizeof(client->client_id) - 1);
1092   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_ip, client_ip, sizeof(client->client_ip) - 1);
1093   client->port = 0; // WebRTC doesn't use port numbers
1094   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, true);
1095   client->server_ctx = server_ctx; // Store server context for receive thread cleanup
1096   log_info("Added new WebRTC client %s from %s (transport=%p, slot=%d)", new_client_id, client_ip, transport, slot);
1097   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, false);
1098   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources, 0); // Render thread updates this
1099   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, 0);     // Send thread updates this
1100   log_debug("WebRTC client slot assigned: client_id=%s assigned to slot %d", client->client_id, slot);
1101   client->connected_at = time(NULL);
1102 
1103   // Initialize crypto context for this client
1104   memset(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
1105   log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Setting crypto_initialized=false (will be set to true after handshake completes)");
1106   // Do NOT set crypto_initialized = true here! The handshake must complete first.
1107   // For WebSocket clients: websocket_client_handler will perform the handshake
1108   // For WebRTC clients: ACDS signaling may have already done crypto (future enhancement)
1109   client->crypto_initialized = false;
1110 
1111   // Initialize pending packet storage (unused for WebRTC, but keep for consistency)
1112   client->pending_packet_type = 0;
1113   client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
1114   client->pending_packet_length = 0;
1115 
1116   // Use noun-based client name for display_name as well
1117   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->display_name, new_client_id, sizeof(client->display_name) - 1);
1118 
1119   // Create individual video buffer for this client using modern double-buffering
1120   client->incoming_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
1121   if (!client->incoming_video_buffer) {
1122     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create video buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1123     log_error("Failed to create video buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1124     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1125   }
1126 
1127   // Create individual audio buffer for this client
1128   client->incoming_audio_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
1129   if (!client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1130     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create audio buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1131     log_error("Failed to create audio buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1132     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1133   }
1134 
1135   // Create packet queues for outgoing data
1136   client->audio_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(500, 1000, false);
1137   if (!client->audio_queue) {
1138     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create audio queue for WebRTC client");
1139     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1140   }
1141 
1142   // Create packet queue for async dispatch: received packets waiting to be processed
1143   // Capacity of 100 packets allows buffering of ~10-50 frames (depending on fragmentation)
1144   client->received_packet_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(100, 500, false);
1145   if (!client->received_packet_queue) {
1146     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create received packet queue for client");
1147     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1148   }
1149 
1150   // Create outgoing video buffer for ASCII frames (double buffered, no dropping)
1151   client->outgoing_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
1152   if (!client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
1153     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create outgoing video buffer for WebRTC client");
1154     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1155   }
1156 
1157   // Pre-allocate send buffer to avoid malloc/free in send thread (prevents deadlocks)
1158   client->send_buffer_size = MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE; // 2MB should handle largest frames
1159   client->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(client->send_buffer_size, 64, void *);
1160   if (!client->send_buffer) {
1161     log_error("Failed to allocate send buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1162     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1163   }
1164 
1165   g_client_manager.client_count = existing_count + 1; // We just added a client
1166   log_debug("Client count updated: now %d clients (added WebRTC client_id=%s to slot %d)",
1167             g_client_manager.client_count, new_client_id, slot);
1168 
1169   // Add client to uthash table for O(1) lookup
1170   HASH_ADD_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, client);
1171   log_debug("Added WebRTC client %s to uthash table", new_client_id);
1172 
1173   // Release the write lock IMMEDIATELY after adding to hash table
1174   // All subsequent operations (mutex init, mixer registration) don't need global client manager lock
1175   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1176 
1177   // Register this client's audio buffer with the mixer
1178   if (g_audio_mixer && client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1179     if (mixer_add_source(g_audio_mixer, new_client_id, client->incoming_audio_buffer) < 0) {
1180       log_warn("Failed to add WebRTC client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
1181     } else {
1182 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
1183       log_debug("Added WebRTC client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
1184 #endif
1185     }
1186   }
1187 
1188   // Initialize mutexes BEFORE creating any threads to prevent race conditions
1189   if (mutex_init(&client->client_state_mutex, "client_state") != 0) {
1190     log_error("Failed to initialize client state mutex for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1191     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1192     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
1193     return NULL;
1194   }
1195 
1196   // Initialize send mutex to protect concurrent socket writes
1197   if (mutex_init(&client->send_mutex, "client_send") != 0) {
1198     log_error("Failed to initialize send mutex for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1199     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1200     remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id);
1201     return NULL;
1202   }
1203 
1204   // Initialize condition variable for dispatch thread wake-up
1205   if (cond_init(&client->dispatch_queue_cond, "dispatch_queue_cond") != 0) {
1206     log_error("Failed to initialize dispatch queue condition variable for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1207     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1208     remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id);
1209     return NULL;
1210   }
1211 
1212   // For WebRTC clients, the capabilities packet will be received by the receive thread
1213   // when it starts. Unlike TCP clients where we handle it synchronously in add_client(),
1214   // WebRTC uses the transport abstraction which handles packet reception automatically.
1215   log_debug("WebRTC client %s initialized - receive thread will process capabilities", new_client_id);
1216 
1217   // Conditionally start threads based on caller preference
1218   // WebSocket handler passes start_threads=false to defer thread startup until after crypto init
1219   // This ensures receive thread doesn't try to process packets before crypto context is ready
1220   if (start_threads) {
1221     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Starting client threads (receive, render) for client %s...", new_client_id);
1222     if (start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, false) != 0) {
1223       log_error("Failed to start threads for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1224       return NULL;
1225     }
1226     log_debug("Created receive thread for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1227     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Receive thread started - thread will now be processing packets", new_client_id);
1228   } else {
1229     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Deferring thread startup for client %s (caller will start after crypto init)",
1230               new_client_id);
1231   }
1232 
1233   // Send initial server state to the new client
1234   if (send_server_state_to_client(client) != 0) {
1235     log_warn("Failed to send initial server state to WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1236   } else {
1237 #ifdef DEBUG_NETWORK
1238     log_info("Sent initial server state to WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1239 #endif
1240   }
1241 
1242   // Send initial server state via ACIP transport
1243   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1244   uint32_t connected_count = g_client_manager.client_count;
1245   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1246 
1247   server_state_packet_t state;
1248   state.connected_client_count = connected_count;
1249   state.active_client_count = 0; // Will be updated by broadcast thread
1250   memset(state.reserved, 0, sizeof(state.reserved));
1251 
1252   // Convert to network byte order
1253   server_state_packet_t net_state;
1254   net_state.connected_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.connected_client_count);
1255   net_state.active_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.active_client_count);
1256   memset(net_state.reserved, 0, sizeof(net_state.reserved));
1257 
1258   asciichat_error_t packet_send_result = acip_send_server_state(client->transport, &net_state);
1259   if (packet_send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1260     log_warn("Failed to send initial server state to WebRTC client %s: %s", new_client_id,
1261              asciichat_error_string(packet_send_result));
1262   } else {
1263     log_debug("Sent initial server state to WebRTC client %s: %u connected clients", new_client_id,
1264               state.connected_client_count);
1265   }
1266 
1267   // Register client with session_host (for discovery mode support)
1268   // WebRTC clients use INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE since they don't have a TCP socket
1269   if (server_ctx->session_host) {
1270     client->session_client_id = session_host_add_client(server_ctx->session_host, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, client_ip, 0);
1271     if (client->session_client_id == 0) {
1272       log_warn("Failed to register WebRTC client %s with session_host", new_client_id);
1273     } else {
1274       log_debug("WebRTC client %s registered with session_host as %u", new_client_id, client->session_client_id);
1275     }
1276   }
1277 
1278   // Broadcast server state to ALL clients AFTER the new client is fully set up
1279   // This notifies all clients (including the new one) about the updated grid
1280   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1281 
1282   return client;
1283 
1284 error_cleanup_webrtc:
1285   // Clean up all partially allocated resources for WebRTC client
1286   // NOTE: This label is reached when allocation or initialization fails BEFORE
1287   // the client is added to the hash table. Don't call remove_client() here.
1288   cleanup_client_all_buffers(client);
1289   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1290   return NULL;
1291 }

    atomic_load_bool(), atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_u64(), audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture(),

void
  broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients (void )


Notify all clients of state changes.


Definition at line 2490 of file
  src/server/client.c.


2490                                                  {
2491   // SNAPSHOT PATTERN: Collect client data while holding lock, then release before network I/O
2492   typedef struct {
2493     socket_t socket;
2494     char client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
2495     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx;
2496   } client_snapshot_t;
2497 
2498   client_snapshot_t client_snapshots[MAX_CLIENTS];
2499   int snapshot_count = 0;
2500   int active_video_count = 0;
2501 
2502   uint64_t lock_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2503   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2504   uint64_t lock_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2505   uint64_t lock_time_ns = time_elapsed_ns(lock_start_ns, lock_end_ns);
2506   if (lock_time_ns > 1 * NS_PER_MS_INT) {
2507     char duration_str[32];
2508     time_pretty((uint64_t)((double)lock_time_ns), -1, duration_str, sizeof(duration_str));
2509     log_warn("broadcast_server_state: rwlock_rdlock took %s", duration_str);
2510   }
2511 
2512   // Count active clients and snapshot client data while holding lock
2513   // Use atomic_load for all atomic fields to prevent data races.
2514   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2515     bool is_active = atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active);
2516     if (is_active && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].is_sending_video)) {
2517       active_video_count++;
2518     }
2519     if (is_active && g_client_manager.clients[i].socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2520       // Skip clients that haven't completed crypto handshake yet
2521       // Check both crypto_initialized AND crypto context (defense in depth)
2522       if (!GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && !g_client_manager.clients[i].crypto_initialized) {
2523         log_debug("Skipping server_state broadcast to client %s: crypto handshake not complete",
2524                   g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id);
2525         continue;
2526       }
2527 
2528       // Get crypto context (may be NULL if --no-encrypt)
2529       const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx =
2530           crypto_handshake_get_context(&g_client_manager.clients[i].crypto_handshake_ctx);
2531       if (!GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && !crypto_ctx) {
2532         // Skip clients without valid crypto context
2533         log_debug("Skipping server_state broadcast to client %s: no crypto context",
2534                   g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id);
2535         continue;
2536       }
2537 
2538       client_snapshots[snapshot_count].socket = g_client_manager.clients[i].socket;
2539       SAFE_STRNCPY(client_snapshots[snapshot_count].client_id, g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id,
2540                    sizeof(client_snapshots[snapshot_count].client_id) - 1);
2541       client_snapshots[snapshot_count].crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
2542       snapshot_count++;
2543     }
2544   }
2545 
2546   // Prepare server state packet while still holding lock (fast operation)
2547   server_state_packet_t state;
2548   state.connected_client_count = g_client_manager.client_count;
2549   state.active_client_count = active_video_count;
2550   memset(state.reserved, 0, sizeof(state.reserved));
2551 
2552   // Convert to network byte order
2553   server_state_packet_t net_state;
2554   net_state.connected_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.connected_client_count);
2555   net_state.active_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.active_client_count);
2556   memset(net_state.reserved, 0, sizeof(net_state.reserved));
2557 
2558   // Release lock BEFORE sending (snapshot pattern)
2559   // Sending while holding lock blocks all client operations
2560   uint64_t lock_held_final_ns = time_get_ns();
2561   uint64_t lock_held_ns = time_elapsed_ns(lock_start_ns, lock_held_final_ns);
2562   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2563 
2564   // Send to all clients AFTER releasing the lock
2565   // This prevents blocking other threads during network I/O
2566   for (int i = 0; i < snapshot_count; i++) {
2567     log_debug_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2568                     "BROADCAST_DEBUG: Sending SERVER_STATE to client %s (socket %d) with crypto_ctx=%p",
2569                     client_snapshots[i].client_id, client_snapshots[i].socket, (void *)client_snapshots[i].crypto_ctx);
2570 
2571     // Protect socket write with per-client send_mutex.
2572     client_info_t *target = find_client_by_id(client_snapshots[i].client_id);
2573     if (target) {
2574       // IMPORTANT: Verify client_id matches expected value - prevents use-after-free
2575       // if client was removed and replaced with another client in same slot
2576       if (strcmp(target->client_id, client_snapshots[i].client_id) != 0) {
2577         log_warn("Client %s ID mismatch during broadcast (found %s), skipping send", client_snapshots[i].client_id,
2578                  target->client_id);
2579         continue;
2580       }
2581 
2582       mutex_lock(&target->send_mutex);
2583 
2584       // Double-check client_id again after acquiring mutex (stronger protection)
2585       if (strcmp(target->client_id, client_snapshots[i].client_id) != 0) {
2586         mutex_unlock(&target->send_mutex);
2587         log_warn("Client %s was removed during broadcast send (now %s), skipping", client_snapshots[i].client_id,
2588                  target->client_id);
2589         continue;
2590       }
2591 
2592       // Send via ACIP transport
2593       asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_server_state(target->transport, &net_state);
2594       mutex_unlock(&target->send_mutex);
2595 
2596       if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2597         log_error("Failed to send server state to client %s: %s", client_snapshots[i].client_id,
2598                   asciichat_error_string(result));
2599       } else {
2600         log_debug_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "Sent server state to client %s: %u connected, %u active",
2601                         client_snapshots[i].client_id, state.connected_client_count, state.active_client_count);
2602       }
2603     } else {
2604       log_warn("Client %s removed before broadcast send could complete", client_snapshots[i].client_id);
2605     }
2606   }
2607 
2608   if (lock_held_ns > 1 * NS_PER_MS_INT) {
2609     char duration_str[32];
2610     time_pretty((uint64_t)((double)lock_held_ns), -1, duration_str, sizeof(duration_str));
2611     log_warn("broadcast_server_state: rwlock held for %s (includes network I/O)", duration_str);
2612   }
2613 }






References acip_send_server_state(),
    atomic_load_bool(), crypto_handshake_get_context(),
    find_client_by_id(), g_client_manager,

void
  * client_dispatch_thread (void * arg)


Async dispatch thread for WebRTC clients. Async dispatch thread -
    processes queued received packets.




Decouples receive from dispatch to prevent backpressure on the
    network socket. Received packets are queued by the receive thread, and
    processed asynchronously here. This allows the receive thread to keep
    accepting packets while dispatch processes them.


Definition at line 1598 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1598                                         {
1599   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
1600 
1601   if (!client) {
1602     log_error("Invalid client info in dispatch thread (NULL pointer)");
1603     return NULL;
1604   }
1605 
1606   const char *client_id = client->client_id;
1607   log_info("DISPATCH_THREAD: Started for client %s", client_id);
1608 
1609   uint64_t dispatch_loop_count = 0;
1610 
1611   while (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && atomic_load_bool(&client->dispatch_thread_running)) {
1612     dispatch_loop_count++;
1613     // Try to dequeue next packet (non-blocking)
1614     // Use try_dequeue to avoid blocking - allows checking exit flag frequently
1615     uint64_t dequeue_start = time_get_ns();
1616     queued_packet_t *queued_pkt = packet_queue_try_dequeue(client->received_packet_queue);
1617     uint64_t dequeue_end = time_get_ns();
1618 
1619     if (!queued_pkt) {
1620       // Queue was empty, wait for signal from receive thread or timeout
1621       // Use 100ms timeout to handle cases where signal is missed due to race conditions
1622       log_dev_every(5 * US_PER_MS_INT, "🔄 DISPATCH_LOOP[%llu]: Queue empty after %.1fμs, waiting for signal",
1623                     (unsigned long long)dispatch_loop_count, (dequeue_end - dequeue_start) / 1000.0);
1624       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1625       cond_timedwait(&client->dispatch_queue_cond, &client->client_state_mutex, 100 * 1000 * 1000); // 100ms timeout
1626       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1627       continue;
1628     }
1629 
1630     // Frame received! Log it immediately
1631     char dequeue_elapsed_str[32];
1632     time_pretty(dequeue_end - dequeue_start, -1, dequeue_elapsed_str, sizeof(dequeue_elapsed_str));
1633     log_info("✅ DISPATCH_THREAD[%u] DEQUEUED packet: %zu bytes (dequeue took %s)", client_id, queued_pkt->data_len,
1634              dequeue_elapsed_str);
1635 
1636     // Process the dequeued packet

1641     size_t payload_len = 0;
1642 
1643     log_info("🔍 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Processing %zu byte packet (header size=%zu)", client_id, total_len,
1644              sizeof(packet_header_t));
1645 
1646     if (total_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1647       log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Packet too small (%zu < %zu), DROPPING", client_id, total_len,
1648                 sizeof(packet_header_t));
1649       packet_queue_free_packet(queued_pkt);
1650       continue;
1651     }
1652 
1653     {
1654       packet_type_t packet_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
1655       payload_len = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->length);
1656 
1657       log_info("🎯 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Packet type=%d, payload_len=%u, total_len=%zu", client_id, packet_type,
1658                payload_len, total_len);
1659 
1660       // Handle PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED from WebSocket clients that encrypt at application layer
1661       // This mirrors the decryption logic in acip_server_receive_and_dispatch()
1662       if (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED && client->transport && client->transport->crypto_ctx) {
1663         log_info("🔐 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Decrypting PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED", client_id);
1664 
1665         uint8_t *ciphertext = payload;
1666         size_t ciphertext_len = payload_len;
1667 
1668         // Decrypt to get inner plaintext packet (header + payload)
1669         size_t plaintext_size = ciphertext_len + 1024;
1670         uint8_t *plaintext = SAFE_MALLOC(plaintext_size, uint8_t *);
1671         if (!plaintext) {
1672           log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Failed to allocate plaintext buffer for decryption", client_id);
1673           packet_queue_free_packet(queued_pkt);
1674           continue;
1675         }
1676 
1677         size_t plaintext_len;
1678         crypto_result_t crypto_result = crypto_decrypt(client->transport->crypto_ctx, ciphertext, ciphertext_len,
1679                                                        plaintext, plaintext_size, &plaintext_len);
1680 
1681         if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
1682           log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Failed to decrypt packet: %s", client_id,
1683                     crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
1684           SAFE_FREE(plaintext);
1685           packet_queue_free_packet(queued_pkt);
1686           continue;
1687         }
1688 
1689         if (plaintext_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1690           log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Decrypted packet too small: %zu < %zu", client_id, plaintext_len,
1691                     sizeof(packet_header_t));
1692           SAFE_FREE(plaintext);
1693           packet_queue_free_packet(queued_pkt);
1694           continue;
1695         }
1696 
1697         // Parse the inner (decrypted) header
1698         const packet_header_t *inner_header = (const packet_header_t *)plaintext;
1699         packet_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(inner_header->type);
1700         payload_len = NET_TO_HOST_U32(inner_header->length);
1701         payload = plaintext + sizeof(packet_header_t);
1702 
1703         log_info("🔐 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Decrypted inner packet type=%d, payload_len=%u", client_id, packet_type,
1704                  payload_len);
1705 
1706         // Dispatch the decrypted packet
1707         if (client->transport) {
1708           log_info("🎯 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Calling acip_handle_server_packet(type=%d, payload_len=%u)", client_id,
1709                    packet_type, payload_len);
1710           asciichat_error_t dispatch_result = acip_handle_server_packet(client->transport, packet_type, payload,
1711                                                                         payload_len, client, &g_acip_server_callbacks);
1712 
1713           if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1714             log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Handler failed for decrypted packet type=%d: %s", client_id, packet_type,
1715                       asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1716           } else {
1717             log_info("✅ DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Successfully dispatched decrypted packet type=%d", client_id,
1718                      packet_type);
1719           }
1720         } else {
1721           log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Cannot dispatch decrypted packet - transport is NULL", client_id);

1724         // Free the decrypted buffer
1725         SAFE_FREE(plaintext);

1728         if (client->transport) {

void
  * client_receive_thread (void * arg)


Definition at line 1754 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1754                                        {
1755   // Log thread startup
1756   log_debug("RECV_THREAD: Thread function entered, arg=%p", arg);
1757 
1758   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
1759 
1760   // Validate client pointer immediately before any access.
1761   // This prevents crashes if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct
1762   // while the thread was still starting at RtlUserThreadStart.
1763   if (!client) {
1764     log_error("Invalid client info in receive thread (NULL pointer)");
1765     return NULL;
1766   }
1767 
1768   // Save this thread's ID so remove_client() can detect self-joins
1769   client->receive_thread_id = asciichat_thread_self();
1770 
1771   log_debug("RECV_THREAD_DEBUG: Thread started, client=%p, client_id=%s, is_tcp=%d", (void *)client, client->client_id,
1772             client->is_tcp_client);
1773 
1774   if (atomic_load_bool(&client->protocol_disconnect_requested)) {
1775     log_debug("Receive thread for client %s exiting before start (protocol disconnect requested)", client->client_id);
1776     return NULL;
1777   }
1778 
1779   // Check if client_id is empty (client struct has been zeroed by remove_client)
1780   // This must be checked BEFORE accessing any client fields
1781   if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
1782     log_debug("Receive thread: client_id is empty, client struct may have been zeroed, exiting");
1783     return NULL;
1784   }
1785 
1786   // Additional validation: check socket is valid
1787   // For TCP clients, validate socket. WebRTC clients use DataChannel (no socket)
1788   if (client->is_tcp_client && client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1789     log_error("Invalid client socket in receive thread");
1790     return NULL;
1791   }
1792 
1793   // Enable thread cancellation for clean shutdown
1794   // Thread cancellation not available in platform abstraction
1795   // Threads should exit when g_should_exit is set
1796 
1797   log_debug("Started receive thread for client %s (%s)", client->client_id, client->display_name);
1798 
1799   // Main receive loop - processes packets from transport
1800   // For TCP clients: receives from socket
1801   // For WebRTC clients: receives from transport ringbuffer (via ACDS signaling)
1802 
1803   log_info("RECV_THREAD_LOOP_START: client_id=%s, is_tcp=%d, transport=%p, active=%d", client->client_id,
1804            client->is_tcp_client, (void *)client->transport, atomic_load_bool(&client->active));
1805 
1806   while (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && atomic_load_bool(&client->active)) {
1807     // For TCP clients, check socket validity
1808     // For WebRTC clients, continue even if no socket (transport handles everything)
1809     if (client->is_tcp_client && client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1810       log_debug("TCP client %s has invalid socket, exiting receive thread", client->client_id);
1811       break;
1812     }
1813 
1814     // Check client_id is still valid before accessing transport.
1815     // This prevents accessing freed memory if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct.
1816     if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
1817       log_debug("Client client_id reset, exiting receive thread");
1818       break;
1819     }
1820 
1821     // Receive packet (without dispatching) - decouple from dispatch for async processing
1822     // For WebRTC clients with async dispatch, we queue packets instead of processing immediately
1823     // This prevents backpressure on the network socket
1824 
1825     if (client->is_tcp_client) {
1826       // TCP clients: use original synchronous dispatch
1827       asciichat_error_t acip_result =
1828           acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(client->transport, client, &g_acip_server_callbacks);
1829 
1830       // Check if shutdown was requested during the network call
1831       if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
1832         log_debug("RECV_EXIT: Server shutdown requested, breaking loop");
1833         break;
1834       }
1835 
1836       // Handle receive errors
1837       if (acip_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1838         asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1839         if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1840           log_error("🔴 ACIP error for client %s: code=%u msg=%s", client->client_id, err_ctx.code,
1841                     err_ctx.context_message);
1842           if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) {
1843             log_debug("Client %s disconnected (network error): %s", client->client_id, err_ctx.context_message);
1844             break;
1845           } else if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_CRYPTO) {
1846             log_error_client(
1847                 client, "SECURITY VIOLATION: Unencrypted packet when encryption required - terminating connection");
1848             atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
1849             break;
1850           }
1851         }
1852         log_warn("ACIP error for TCP client %s: %s (disconnecting)", client->client_id,
1853                  asciichat_error_string(acip_result));
1854         break;
1855       }
1856     } else {
1857       // WebRTC/WebSocket clients: async dispatch - receive packet and queue for async processing
1858       void *packet_data = NULL;
1859       void *allocated_buffer = NULL;
1860       size_t packet_len = 0;
1861 
1862       // Snapshot transport pointer to prevent use-after-free
1863       // Transport can be destroyed by WebSocket callback while we're receiving
1864       acip_transport_t *transport_snapshot = NULL;
1865       mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
1866       if (!client->transport || atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
1867         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
1868         log_debug("RECV_THREAD[%s]: Transport destroyed or client shutting down, exiting receive loop",
1869                   client->client_id);
1870         break;
1871       }
1872       transport_snapshot = client->transport;
1873       mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
1874 
1875       log_debug("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: About to call transport->recv() (transport=%p)", client->client_id,
1876                 (void *)transport_snapshot);
1877 
1878       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1879           transport_snapshot->methods->recv(transport_snapshot, &packet_data, &packet_len, &allocated_buffer);
1880 
1881       // Check error BEFORE logging - logging system may clear thread-local errno
1882       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1883         asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1884         if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1885           // Check for reassembly timeout (fragments arriving slowly)
1886           // This is NOT a connection failure - safe to retry
1887           if ((err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) && err_ctx.context_message &&
1888               strstr(err_ctx.context_message, "reassembly timeout")) {
1889             // Fragments are arriving slowly - this is normal, retry without disconnecting
1890             log_dev_every(100000, "Client %s: fragment reassembly timeout, retrying in 10ms", client->client_id);
1891             platform_sleep_ms(10); // Sleep 10ms to allow fragments to arrive
1892             continue;              // Retry without disconnecting
1893           }
1894 
1895           if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) {
1896             log_debug("Client %s disconnected (network error): %s", client->client_id, err_ctx.context_message);
1897             break;
1898           }
1899         }
1900         log_warn("Receive failed for WebRTC client %s: %s (disconnecting)", client->client_id,
1901                  asciichat_error_string(recv_result));
1902         break;
1903       }
1904 
1905       log_info("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: transport->recv() returned result=%d, packet_len=%zu, allocated_buffer=%p",
1906                client->client_id, recv_result, packet_len, allocated_buffer);
1907 
1908       // Validate received packet before queueing
1909       if (packet_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1910         log_warn("RECV_THREAD[%s]: Received packet too small (%zu < %zu), dropping", client->client_id, packet_len,
1911                  sizeof(packet_header_t));
1912         if (allocated_buffer) {
1913           buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
1914         }
1915         continue;
1916       }
1917 
1918       // Queue the received packet for async dispatch
1919       // This prevents the receive thread from blocking on dispatch
1920       log_info("✅ RECV_THREAD[%s]: Queuing %zu byte packet for async dispatch", client->client_id, packet_len);
1921 
1922       // Extract packet type from the header to preserve it when queueing
1923       const packet_header_t *pkt_header = (const packet_header_t *)allocated_buffer;
1924       packet_type_t pkt_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(pkt_header->type);
1925       uint32_t payload_len = NET_TO_HOST_U32(pkt_header->length);
1926 
1927       log_info("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: Packet header: type=%d, payload_len=%u, total_len=%zu", client->client_id, pkt_type,
1928                payload_len, packet_len);
1929 
1930       // Build a complete packet to queue (header + payload)
1931       // The entire buffer (allocated_buffer) contains the full packet
1932       // copy_data=false because we want to transfer ownership to the queue
1933       uint32_t client_id_hash = fnv1a_hash_string(client->client_id);
1934       int enqueue_result = packet_queue_enqueue(client->received_packet_queue, pkt_type, allocated_buffer, packet_len,
1935                                                 client_id_hash, false);
1936 
1937       if (enqueue_result < 0) {
1938         log_error("🔴 RECV_THREAD[%s]: Failed to queue received packet (queue full?) - DROPPING FRAME",
1939                   client->client_id);
1940         if (allocated_buffer) {
1941           buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
1942         }
1943       } else {
1944         log_info("✅ RECV_THREAD[%s]: Successfully queued packet (type=%d, len=%zu)", client->client_id, pkt_type,
1945                  packet_len);
1946         // Signal dispatch thread to wake up and process the newly queued packet
1947         cond_signal(&client->dispatch_queue_cond);
1948       }
1949     }
1950   }
1951 
1952   // Mark client as inactive and stop all threads
1953   // Must stop render threads when client disconnects.
1954   // OPTIMIZED: Use atomic operations for thread control flags (lock-free)
1955   const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;
1956   log_debug("Setting active=false in receive_thread_fn (client_id=%s, exiting receive loop)", client_id_snapshot);
1957   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
1958   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
1959   atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
1960   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, false);
1961 
1962   // Call remove_client() to trigger cleanup
1963   // Safe to call from receive thread now: remove_client() detects self-join via thread IDs
1964   // and skips the receive thread join when called from the receive thread itself
1965   log_debug("Receive thread for client %s calling remove_client() for cleanup", client_id_snapshot);
1966   server_context_t *server_ctx = (server_context_t *)client->server_ctx;
1967   if (server_ctx) {
1968     if (remove_client(server_ctx, client_id_snapshot) != 0) {
1969       log_warn("Failed to remove client %s from receive thread cleanup", client_id_snapshot);
1970     }
1971   } else {
1972     log_error("Receive thread for client %s: server_ctx is NULL, cannot call remove_client()", client_id_snapshot);
1973   }
1974 
1975   log_debug("Receive thread for client %s terminated", client_id_snapshot);
1976 
1977   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
1978   asciichat_errno_destroy();
1979 
1980   return NULL;
1981 }

    atomic_load_bool(), atomic_store_bool(),
    buffer_pool_free(), g_should_exit,

void
  * client_send_thread_func (void * arg)

1985   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
1986 
1987   // Validate client pointer immediately before any access.

1991     log_error("Invalid client info in send thread (NULL pointer)");

1994

1996   // This must be checked BEFORE accessing any client fields
