2012            client->client_id, has_socket, has_transport, (void *)client->transport);
2013 
2014   if (!has_socket && !has_transport) {
2015     log_error("Invalid client connection in send thread (no socket or transport)");
2016     return NULL;
2017   }
2018 
2019   log_info("Started send thread for client %s (%s)", client->client_id, client->display_name);
2020 
2021   // Mark thread as running
2022   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, true);
2023 
2024   log_info("SEND_THREAD_LOOP_START: client_id=%s active=%d shutting_down=%d running=%d", client->client_id,
2025            atomic_load_bool(&client->active), atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down),
2026            atomic_load_bool(&client->send_thread_running));
2027 
2028   // Track timing for video frame sends
2029   uint64_t last_video_send_time = 0;
2030   const uint64_t video_send_interval_us = 16666; // 60fps = ~16.67ms
2031 
2032   // High-frequency audio loop - separate from video frame loop
2033   // to ensure audio packets are sent immediately, not rate-limited by video
2034 #define MAX_AUDIO_BATCH 8
2035   int loop_iteration_count = 0;
2036   while (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) &&
2037          atomic_load_bool(&client->active) && atomic_load_bool(&client->send_thread_running)) {
2038     loop_iteration_count++;
2039     bool sent_something = false;
2040     uint64_t loop_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2041     log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] START: client=%s", loop_iteration_count, client->client_id);
2042 
2043     // PRIORITY: Drain all queued audio packets before video
2044     // Audio must not be rate-limited by video frame sending (16.67ms)
2045     queued_packet_t *audio_packets[MAX_AUDIO_BATCH];
2046     int audio_packet_count = 0;
2047 
2048     if (client->audio_queue) {
2049       // Try to dequeue multiple audio packets
2050       for (int i = 0; i < MAX_AUDIO_BATCH; i++) {
2051         audio_packets[i] = packet_queue_try_dequeue(client->audio_queue);
2052         if (audio_packets[i]) {
2053           audio_packet_count++;
2054         } else {
2055           break; // No more packets available
2056         }
2057       }
2058       if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2059         log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SEND_AUDIO: client=%s dequeued=%d packets", client->client_id,
2060                       audio_packet_count);
2061       }
2062     } else {
2063       log_warn("Send thread: audio_queue is NULL for client %s", client->client_id);
2064     }
2065 
2066     // Send batched audio if we have packets
2067     if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2068       // Protect crypto field access with mutex
2069       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2070       bool crypto_ready = !GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && client->crypto_initialized &&
2071                           crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
2072       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2073 
2074       // Audio packets cannot be sent until crypto handshake completes (protocol requirement)
2075       if (!crypto_ready) {
2076         log_dev_every(1000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SKIP_AUDIO: client=%s handshake not ready, dropping %d audio packets",
2077                       client->client_id, audio_packet_count);
2078         // Drop audio packets until handshake completes
2079         for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2080           packet_queue_free_packet(audio_packets[i]);
2081         }
2082         audio_packet_count = 0;
2083       }
2084 
2085       asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
2086 
2087       // Only send audio if packets survived crypto check (count > 0 after potential drop)
2088       if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2089         if (audio_packet_count == 1) {
2090           // Single packet - send directly for low latency using ACIP transport
2091           packet_type_t pkt_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(audio_packets[0]->header.type);
2092 
2093           // Get transport reference while holding mutex briefly (prevents deadlock on TCP buffer full)
2094           mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2095           if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2096             mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2097             log_warn("BREAK_AUDIO_SINGLE: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p", client->client_id,
2098                      atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport);
2099             break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2100           }
2101           acip_transport_t *transport = client->transport;
2102           mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2103 
2104           // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex
2105           uint32_t client_id_hash = fnv1a_hash_string(client->client_id);
2106           result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, pkt_type, audio_packets[0]->data, audio_packets[0]->data_len,
2107                                              client_id_hash);
2108           if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2109             log_error("AUDIO SEND FAIL: client=%s, len=%zu, result=%d", client->client_id, audio_packets[0]->data_len,
2110                       result);
2111           }
2112         } else {
2113           // Multiple packets - batch them together and send via transport (works for all client types)
2114           packet_type_t first_pkt_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(audio_packets[0]->header.type);
2115 
2116           // Get transport reference
2117           mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2118           if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2119             mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2120             log_warn("BREAK_AUDIO_BATCH: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p", client->client_id,
2121                      atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport);
2122             result = ERROR_NETWORK;
2123           } else {
2124             acip_transport_t *transport = client->transport;
2125             mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2126 
2127             if (first_pkt_type == PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH) {
2128               // Opus packets - batch and send via transport
2129               size_t total_opus_size = 0;
2130               for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2131                 total_opus_size += audio_packets[i]->data_len;
2132               }
2133 
2134               uint8_t *batched_opus = SAFE_MALLOC(total_opus_size, uint8_t *);
2135               uint16_t *frame_sizes = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)audio_packet_count * sizeof(uint16_t), uint16_t *);
2136 
2137               if (batched_opus && frame_sizes) {
2138                 size_t offset = 0;
2139                 for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2140                   frame_sizes[i] = (uint16_t)audio_packets[i]->data_len;
2141                   memcpy(batched_opus + offset, audio_packets[i]->data, audio_packets[i]->data_len);
2142                   offset += audio_packets[i]->data_len;
2143                 }
2144                 result = acip_send_audio_opus_batch(transport, batched_opus, total_opus_size, frame_sizes,
2145                                                     (uint32_t)audio_packet_count, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE, 20);
2146                 if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2147                   log_error("AUDIO SEND FAIL (opus batch): client=%u, frames=%d, total_size=%zu, result=%d",
2148                             client->client_id, audio_packet_count, total_opus_size, result);
2149                 }
2150               } else {
2151                 log_error("Failed to allocate buffer for Opus batch");
2152                 result = ERROR_MEMORY;
2153               }
2154               SAFE_FREE(batched_opus);
2155               SAFE_FREE(frame_sizes);
2156             } else {
2157               // Raw float audio - batch and send via transport
2158               size_t total_samples = 0;
2159               for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2160                 total_samples += audio_packets[i]->data_len / sizeof(float);
2161               }
2162 
2163               float *batched_audio = SAFE_MALLOC(total_samples * sizeof(float), float *);
2164               if (batched_audio) {
2165                 size_t offset = 0;
2166                 for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2167                   size_t packet_samples = audio_packets[i]->data_len / sizeof(float);
2168                   memcpy(batched_audio + offset, audio_packets[i]->data, audio_packets[i]->data_len);
2169                   offset += packet_samples;
2170                 }
2171                 result = acip_send_audio_batch(transport, batched_audio, (uint32_t)total_samples, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE);
2172                 if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2173                   log_error("AUDIO SEND FAIL (raw batch): client=%u, packets=%d, samples=%zu, result=%d",
2174                             client->client_id, audio_packet_count, total_samples, result);
2175                 }
2176               } else {
2177                 log_error("Failed to allocate buffer for audio batch");
2178                 result = ERROR_MEMORY;
2179               }
2180               SAFE_FREE(batched_audio);
2181             }
2182           }
2183         }
2184       } // End of if (audio_packet_count > 0)
2185 
2186       // Free all audio packets
2187       for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2188         packet_queue_free_packet(audio_packets[i]);
2189       }
2190 
2191       if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2192         if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2193           log_error("Failed to send audio to client %s: %s", client->client_id, asciichat_error_string(result));
2194         }
2195         // Network errors corrupt the TCP stream - must disconnect immediately
2196         if (result == ERROR_NETWORK) {
2197           log_error("CLOSING_CLIENT_ON_NETWORK_ERROR: client_id=%s due to audio send failure (corrupted stream)",
2198                     client->client_id);
2199           break; // Exit send loop to trigger cleanup
2200         }
2201         log_warn("SKIP_AUDIO_ERROR: client_id=%s result=%d (continuing to send video)", client->client_id, result);
2202         // Continue sending video for non-network errors (audio is optional for browser clients)
2203       }
2204 
2205       sent_something = true;
2206       uint64_t audio_done_ns = time_get_ns();
2207       char audio_elapsed_str[32];
2208       time_pretty(audio_done_ns - loop_start_ns, -1, audio_elapsed_str, sizeof(audio_elapsed_str));
2209       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] AUDIO_SENT: took %s", loop_iteration_count,
2210                      audio_elapsed_str);
2211 
2212       // Small sleep to let more audio packets queue (helps batching efficiency)
2213       if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2214         platform_sleep_us(100); // 0.1ms - minimal delay
2215       }
2216     } else {
2217       // No audio packets - brief sleep to avoid busy-looping, then check for other tasks
2218       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] NO_AUDIO: sleeping 1ms", loop_iteration_count);
2219       platform_sleep_us(1 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 1ms - enough for audio render thread to queue more packets
2220 
2221       // Check if session rekeying should be triggered
2222       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2223       bool should_rekey = !GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && client->crypto_initialized &&
2224                           crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx) &&
2225                           crypto_handshake_should_rekey(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
2226       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2227 
2228       if (should_rekey) {
2229         log_debug("Rekey threshold reached for client %s, initiating session rekey", client->client_id);
2230         mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2231         // Get socket reference briefly to avoid deadlock on TCP buffer full
2232         mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2233         if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2234           mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2235           mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2236           log_warn("BREAK_REKEY: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d socket=%d", client->client_id,
2237                    atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (int)client->socket);
2238           break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2239         }
2240         socket_t rekey_socket = client->socket;
2241         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2242 
2243         // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the send_mutex (client_state_mutex still held for crypto state)
2244         asciichat_error_t result = crypto_handshake_rekey_request(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, rekey_socket);
2245         mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2246 
2247         if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2248           log_error("Failed to send REKEY_REQUEST to client %s: %d", client->client_id, result);
2249         } else {
2250           log_debug("Sent REKEY_REQUEST to client %s", client->client_id);
2251           // Notify client that session rekeying has been initiated (old keys still active)
2252           log_info_client(client, "Session rekey initiated - rotating encryption keys");
2253         }
2254       }
2255     }
2256 
2257     // Always consume frames from the buffer to prevent accumulation
2258     // Rate-limit the actual sending, but always mark frames as consumed
2259     uint64_t video_check_ns = time_get_ns();
2260     if (!client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
2261       // Buffer has been destroyed (client is shutting down).
2262       // Exit cleanly instead of looping forever trying to access freed memory.
2263       log_warn("⚠️  Send thread exiting: outgoing_video_buffer is NULL for client %s (client shutting down?)",
2264                client->client_id);
2265       break;
2266     }
2267 
2268     // Get latest frame from double buffer (lock-free operation)
2269     // This marks the frame as consumed even if we don't send it yet
2270     const video_frame_t *frame = video_frame_get_latest(client->outgoing_video_buffer);
2271     uint64_t frame_get_ns = time_get_ns();
2272     char frame_get_elapsed_str[32];
2273     time_pretty(frame_get_ns - video_check_ns, -1, frame_get_elapsed_str, sizeof(frame_get_elapsed_str));
2274     log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] VIDEO_GET_FRAME: took %s, frame=%p", loop_iteration_count,
2275                    frame_get_elapsed_str, (void *)frame);
2276     log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "Send thread: video_frame_get_latest returned %p for client %s", (void *)frame,
2277                   client->client_id);
2278 
2279     // Check if get_latest failed (buffer might have been destroyed)
2280     if (!frame) {
2281       log_warn("⚠️  Send thread exiting: video_frame_get_latest returned NULL for client %s (buffer destroyed?)",
2282                client->client_id);
2283       break; // Exit thread if buffer is invalid
2284     }
2285 
2286     // Check if it's time to send a video frame (60fps rate limiting)
2287     // Only rate-limit the SEND operation, not frame consumption
2288     uint64_t current_time_ns = time_get_ns();
2289     uint64_t current_time_us = time_ns_to_us(current_time_ns);
2290     uint64_t time_since_last_send_us = current_time_us - last_video_send_time;
2291     log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2292                   "Send thread timing check: time_since_last=%llu us, interval=%llu us, should_send=%d",
2293                   (unsigned long long)time_since_last_send_us, (unsigned long long)video_send_interval_us,
2294                   (time_since_last_send_us >= video_send_interval_us));
2295 
2296     if (current_time_us - last_video_send_time >= video_send_interval_us) {
2297       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✓ SEND_TIME_READY: client_id=%s time_since=%llu interval=%llu",
2298                      client->client_id, (unsigned long long)time_since_last_send_us,
2299                      (unsigned long long)video_send_interval_us);
2300       uint64_t frame_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2301 
2302       // GRID LAYOUT CHANGE: Check if render thread has buffered a frame with different source count
2303       // If so, send CLEAR_CONSOLE before sending the new frame
2304       int rendered_sources = atomic_load_bool(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources);
2305       int sent_sources = atomic_load_bool(&client->last_sent_grid_sources);
2306 
2307       if (rendered_sources != sent_sources && rendered_sources > 0) {
2308         // Grid layout changed! Send CLEAR_CONSOLE before next frame using ACIP transport
2309         // Get transport reference briefly to avoid deadlock on TCP buffer full
2310         mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2311         if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2312           mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2313           log_warn("BREAK_CLEAR_CONSOLE: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p", client->client_id,
2314                    atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport);
2315           break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2316         }
2317         acip_transport_t *clear_transport = client->transport;
2318         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2319 
2320         // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex
2321         acip_send_clear_console(clear_transport);
2322         log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_FAST, "Client %s: Sent CLEAR_CONSOLE (grid changed %d → %d sources)",
2323                         client->client_id, sent_sources, rendered_sources);
2324         atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, rendered_sources);
2325         sent_something = true;
2326       }
2327 
2328       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2329                      "🎯 FRAME_VALIDATION: client_id=%s frame=%p, frame->data=%p, frame->size=%zu", client->client_id,
2330                      (void *)frame, (void *)frame->data, frame->size);
2331 
2332       if (!frame->data) {
2333         log_info("❌ SKIP_NO_DATA: client_id=%s frame=%p data=%p", client->client_id, (void *)frame,
2334                  (void *)frame->data);
2335         continue;
2336       }
2337       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✅ FRAME_DATA_OK: client_id=%s data=%p", client->client_id,
2338                      (void *)frame->data);
2339 
2340       if (frame->data && frame->size == 0) {
2341         log_info("❌ SKIP_ZERO_SIZE: client_id=%s size=%zu", client->client_id, frame->size);
2342         platform_sleep_us(1 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 1ms sleep
2343         continue;
2344       }
2345       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✅ FRAME_SIZE_OK: client_id=%s size=%zu", client->client_id, frame->size);
2346 
2347       // Snapshot frame metadata (safe with double-buffer system)
2348       const char *frame_data = (const char *)frame->data; // Pointer snapshot - data is stable in front buffer
2349       size_t frame_size = frame->size;                    // Size snapshot - prevent race condition with render thread
2350       uint32_t width = client->width;
2351       uint32_t height = client->height;
2352       uint64_t step1_ns = time_get_ns();
2353       uint64_t step2_ns = time_get_ns();
2354       uint64_t step3_ns = time_get_ns();
2355       uint64_t step4_ns = time_get_ns();
2356 
2357       // Check if crypto handshake is complete before sending (prevents sending to unauthenticated clients)
2358       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2359       bool crypto_ready = GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) ||
2360                           (client->crypto_initialized && crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
2361       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2362 
2363       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2364                      "🔐 CRYPTO_CHECK: client_id=%s crypto_ready=%d crypto_initialized=%d no_encrypt=%d",
2365                      client->client_id, crypto_ready, client->crypto_initialized, GET_OPTION(no_encrypt));
2366 
2367       if (!crypto_ready) {
2368         log_info("⚠️  SKIP_SEND_CRYPTO: client_id=%s crypto_initialized=%d no_encrypt=%d", client->client_id,
2369                  client->crypto_initialized, GET_OPTION(no_encrypt));
2370         continue; // Skip this frame, will try again on next loop iteration
2371       }
2372       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✅ CRYPTO_READY: client_id=%s about to send frame", client->client_id);
2373 
2374       // Get transport reference briefly to avoid deadlock on TCP buffer full
2375       // ACIP transport handles header building, CRC32, encryption internally
2376       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2377                     "Send thread: About to send frame to client %s (width=%u, height=%u, size=%zu, data=%p)",
2378                     client->client_id, width, height, frame_size, (void *)frame_data);
2379 
2380       // Log first 32 bytes of frame data to verify we can access it
2381       if (frame_data && frame_size > 0) {
2382         log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2383                        "FRAME_DATA_HEX: client=%u first_bytes=[%02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x "
2384                        "%02x %02x %02x %02x %02x]",
2385                        client->client_id, ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[0], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[1],
2386                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[2], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[3], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[4],
2387                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[5], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[6], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[7],
2388                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[8], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[9], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[10],
2389                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[11], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[12], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[13],
2390                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[14], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[15]);
2391       }
2392 
2393       mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2394       if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2395         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2396         log_warn("BREAK_FRAME_SEND: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p loop_iter=%d", client->client_id,
2397                  atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport, loop_iteration_count);
2398         break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2399       }
2400       acip_transport_t *frame_transport = client->transport;
2401       mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2402 
2403       // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex
2404       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SEND_ASCII_FRAME: client_id=%s size=%zu width=%u height=%u",
2405                     client->client_id, frame_size, width, height);
2406       uint64_t send_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2407       log_dev("[SEND_LOOP_%d] FRAME_SEND_START: size=%zu", loop_iteration_count, frame_size);
2408       asciichat_error_t send_result =
2409           acip_send_ascii_frame(frame_transport, frame_data, frame_size, width, height, client->client_id);
2410       uint64_t send_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2411       char send_elapsed_str[32];
2412       time_pretty(send_end_ns - send_start_ns, -1, send_elapsed_str, sizeof(send_elapsed_str));
2413       log_dev("[SEND_LOOP_%d] FRAME_SEND_END: took %s, result=%d", loop_iteration_count, send_elapsed_str, send_result);
2414       uint64_t step5_ns = time_get_ns();
2415 
2416       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2417         if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2418           SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send video frame to client %s: %s", client->client_id,
2419                     asciichat_error_string(send_result));
2420         }
2421         log_error("SEND_FRAME_FAILED: client_id=%s result=%d message=%s loop_iter=%d", client->client_id, send_result,
2422                   asciichat_error_string(send_result), loop_iteration_count);
2423         log_warn("BREAK_SEND_ERROR: client_id=%s (frame send failed)", client->client_id);
2424         break;
2425       }
2426 
2427       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SEND_FRAME_SUCCESS: client_id=%s size=%zu", client->client_id, frame_size);
2428 
2429       // Increment frame counter and log
2430       unsigned long frame_count = atomic_fetch_add_u64(&client->frames_sent_count, 1) + 1;
2431       log_info("🎬 FRAME_SENT: client_id=%s frame_num=%lu size=%zu", client->client_id, frame_count, frame_size);
2432 
2433       sent_something = true;
2434       last_video_send_time = current_time_us;
2435 
2436       uint64_t frame_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2437       uint64_t frame_time_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(frame_start_ns, frame_end_ns));
2438       if (frame_time_us > 15 * US_PER_MS_INT) { // Log if sending a frame takes > 15ms (encryption adds ~5-6ms)
2439         uint64_t step1_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(frame_start_ns, step1_ns));
2440         uint64_t step2_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step1_ns, step2_ns));
2441         uint64_t step3_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step2_ns, step3_ns));
2442         uint64_t step4_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step3_ns, step4_ns));
2443         uint64_t step5_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step4_ns, step5_ns));
2444         log_warn_every(
2445             LOG_RATE_DEFAULT,
2446             "SEND_THREAD: Frame send took %.2fms for client %s | Snapshot: %.2fms | Memcpy: %.2fms | CRC32: %.2fms | "
2447             "Header: %.2fms | send_packet_secure: %.2fms",
2448             frame_time_us / 1000.0, client->client_id, step1_us / 1000.0, step2_us / 1000.0, step3_us / 1000.0,
2449             step4_us / 1000.0, step5_us / 1000.0);
2450       }
2451     }
2452 
2453     // If we didn't send anything, sleep briefly to prevent busy waiting
2454     if (!sent_something) {
2455       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] IDLE_SLEEP: nothing sent", loop_iteration_count);
2456       platform_sleep_us(1 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 1ms sleep
2457     }
2458     uint64_t loop_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2459     uint64_t loop_elapsed_ns = loop_end_ns - loop_start_ns;
2460     if (loop_elapsed_ns > 10 * NS_PER_MS) {
2461       char loop_elapsed_str[32];
2462       time_pretty(loop_elapsed_ns, -1, loop_elapsed_str, sizeof(loop_elapsed_str));
2463       log_warn("[SEND_LOOP_%d] SLOW_ITERATION: took %s (client=%u)", loop_iteration_count, loop_elapsed_str,
2464                client->client_id);
2465     }
2466   }
2467 
2468   // Log why the send thread exited
2469   log_warn("Send thread exit conditions - client_id=%s g_should_exit=%d shutting_down=%d active=%d "
2470            "send_thread_running=%d",
2471            client->client_id, atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit), atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down),
2472            atomic_load_bool(&client->active), atomic_load_bool(&client->send_thread_running));
2473 
2474   // Mark thread as stopped
2475   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
2476   log_debug("Send thread for client %s terminated", client->client_id);
2477 
2478   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
2479   asciichat_errno_destroy();
2480 
2481   return NULL;
2482 }






References acip_send_ascii_frame(),
    acip_send_audio_batch(), acip_send_audio_opus_batch(),
    acip_send_clear_console(), asciichat_errno_destroy(),
    atomic_fetch_add_u64(), atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), atomic_store_u64(),
    crypto_handshake_is_ready(), crypto_handshake_rekey_request(),
    crypto_handshake_should_rekey(), frame, g_should_exit,
    MAX_AUDIO_BATCH, packet_queue_free_packet(),
    packet_queue_try_dequeue(), packet_send_via_transport(),
    platform_sleep_us(), time_elapsed_ns(), time_get_ns(),
    time_pretty(), and video_frame_get_latest().






client_info_t
  * find_client_by_id (const char * client_id)


Fast O(1) client lookup by ID using hash table. This is the
    primary method for locating clients throughout the server. It uses a hash
    table for constant-time lookups regardless of client count, making it
    suitable for high-performance operations like rendering and stats.




PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Time Complexity: O(1) average case, O(n) worst case (hash collision)
    • 
  
  • Space Complexity: O(1)
    • 
  
  • Thread Safety: Hash table is internally thread-safe for lookups
    • 



USAGE PATTERNS:


    
  
  • Called by render threads to find target clients for frame generation
    • 
  
  • Used by protocol handlers to locate clients for packet processing
    • 
  
  • Stats collection for per-client performance monitoring
    • 



Parameters


client_id Unique identifier for the client (0 is
  invalid)


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t if found, NULL if not found or
  invalid ID


Note


Does not require external locking - hash table provides
  thread safety



Returns direct pointer to client struct - caller should use
    snapshot pattern




Definition at line 386 of file
  src/server/client.c.


386                                                         {
387   if (!client_id || client_id[0] == '\0') {
388     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid client ID");
389     return NULL;
390   }
391 
392   // Protect uthash lookup with read lock to prevent concurrent access issues
393   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
394 
395   client_info_t *result = NULL;
396   HASH_FIND_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, result);
397 
398   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
399 
400   if (!result) {
401     log_warn("Client not found for ID %s", client_id);
402   }
403 
404   return result;
405 }






References g_client_manager, and
    g_client_manager_rwlock.


Referenced by broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(),
    crypto_server_cleanup_client(),
    crypto_server_decrypt_packet(),
    crypto_server_encrypt_packet(), crypto_server_get_context(),
    crypto_server_is_ready(), and
  start_webrtc_client_threads().






client_info_t
  * find_client_by_socket (socket_t socket)


Find client by socket descriptor using linear search. This
    function provides socket-based client lookup, primarily used during
    connection establishment before client IDs are assigned. Less efficient than
    find_client_by_id() but necessary for socket-based operations.




PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Time Complexity: O(n) where n = number of active clients
    • 
  
  • Space Complexity: O(1)
    • 
  
  • Thread Safety: Internally acquires read lock on
    g_client_manager_rwlock
    • 



USAGE PATTERNS:


    
  
  • Connection establishment during add_client() processing
    • 
  
  • Socket error handling and cleanup operations
    • 
  
  • Debugging and diagnostic functions
    • 



Parameters


socket Platform-abstracted socket descriptor to
  search for


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t if found, NULL if not
  found


Note


Only searches active clients (avoids returning stale
  entries)



Caller should use snapshot pattern when accessing returned client
    data




Definition at line 430 of file
  src/server/client.c.


430                                                       {
431   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
432 
433   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
434     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].socket == socket && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
435       client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
436       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
437       return client;
438     }
439   }
440 
441   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
442   return NULL;
443 }






References atomic_load_bool(), g_client_manager, and
    g_client_manager_rwlock.

client Client info structure


type Packet type


data Packet data


len Packet length

3705     log_debug("CLIENT: client_id=%s, data=%p, len=%zu", client->client_id, data, len);
3706   }
3707 
3708   // Rate limiting: Check and record packet-specific rate limits
3709   if (g_rate_limiter) {
3710     if (!check_and_record_packet_rate_limit(g_rate_limiter, client->client_ip, client->socket, type)) {
3711       // Rate limit exceeded - error response already sent by utility function
3712       return;
3713     }
3714   }
3715 
3716   // O(1) dispatch via hash table lookup
3717   int idx = client_dispatch_hash_lookup(g_client_dispatch_hash, type);
3718   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3719     log_error("DISPATCH_LOOKUP: type=%d, idx=%d (len=%zu)", type, idx, len);
3720   }
3721   if (idx < 0) {
3722     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Unknown packet type: %d (len=%zu)", type, len);
3723     return;
3724   }
3725 
3726   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3727     log_error("DISPATCH_HANDLER: type=%d, calling handler[%d]...", type, idx);
3728   }
3729   g_client_dispatch_handlers[idx](client, data, len);
3730   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3731     log_error("DISPATCH_HANDLER: type=%d, handler returned", type);
3732   }
3733 }

int
  process_encrypted_packet (client_info_t * client, packet_type_t * type, void
  ** data, size_t * len, uint32_t * sender_id)


Process an encrypted packet from a client




Parameters


client Client info structure


type Pointer to packet type (will be updated with decrypted type)


data Pointer to packet data (will be updated with decrypted data)


len Pointer to packet length (will be updated with decrypted length)


sender_id Pointer to sender ID (will be updated with decrypted sender
  ID)


Returns


0 on success, -1 on error

  src/server/client.c.


2768                                                   {
2769   if (!crypto_server_is_ready(client->client_id)) {
2770     log_error("Received encrypted packet but crypto not ready for client %s", client->client_id);
2771     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, *len);
2772     *data = NULL;
2773     return -1;
2774   }
2775 
2776   // Store original allocation size before it gets modified
2777   size_t original_alloc_size = *len;
2778   void *decrypted_data = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, original_alloc_size);
2779   size_t decrypted_len;
2780   int decrypt_result = crypto_server_decrypt_packet(client->client_id, (const uint8_t *)*data, *len,
2781                                                     (uint8_t *)decrypted_data, original_alloc_size, &decrypted_len);
2782 
2783   if (decrypt_result != 0) {
2784     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to process encrypted packet from client %s (result=%d)", client->client_id,
2785               decrypt_result);
2786     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, original_alloc_size);
2787     buffer_pool_free(NULL, decrypted_data, original_alloc_size);
2788     *data = NULL;
2789     return -1;
2790   }
2791 
2792   // Replace encrypted data with decrypted data
2793   // Use original allocation size for freeing the encrypted buffer
2794   buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, original_alloc_size);
2795 
2796   *data = decrypted_data;
2797   *len = decrypted_len;
2798 
2799   // Now process the decrypted packet by parsing its header
2800   if (*len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
2801     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Decrypted packet too small for header from client %s", client->client_id);
2802     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, *len);
2803     *data = NULL;
2804     return -1;
2805   }
2806 
2807   packet_header_t *header = (packet_header_t *)*data;
2808   *type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
2809   *sender_id = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->client_id);
2810 
2811   // Adjust data pointer to skip header
2812   *data = (uint8_t *)*data + sizeof(packet_header_t);
2813   *len -= sizeof(packet_header_t);
2814 
2815   return 0;
2816 }

int
  remove_client (server_context_t * server_ctx, const char *
  client_id)


Definition at line 1293 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1293                                                                        {
1294   if (!server_ctx) {
1295     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Cannot remove client %s: NULL server_ctx", client_id);
1296     return -1;
1297   }
1298 
1299   // Phase 1: Mark client inactive and prepare for cleanup while holding write lock
1300   client_info_t *target_client = NULL;
1301   char display_name_copy[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
1302   socket_t client_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE; // Save socket for thread cleanup
1303 
1304   log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Attempting to remove client %s", client_id);
1305   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1306 
1307   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1308     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
1309     if (strcmp(client->client_id, client_id) == 0 && client->client_id[0] != '\0') {
1310       // Check if already being removed by another thread
1311       // This prevents double-free and use-after-free crashes during concurrent cleanup
1312       if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
1313         rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1314         log_debug("Client %s already being removed by another thread, skipping", client_id);
1315         return 0; // Return success - removal is in progress
1316       }
1317       // Mark as shutting down and inactive immediately to stop new operations
1318       log_debug("Setting active=false in remove_client (client_id=%s, socket=%d)", client_id, client->socket);
1319       log_info("Removing client %s (socket=%d) - marking inactive and clearing video flags", client_id, client->socket);
1320       atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, true);
1321       atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
1322       atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_video, false);
1323       atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_audio, false);
1324       target_client = client;
1325 
1326       // Store display name before clearing
1327       SAFE_STRNCPY(display_name_copy, client->display_name, MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN - 1);
1328 
1329       // Save socket for tcp_server_stop_client_threads() before closing
1330       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1331       client_socket = client->socket; // Save socket for thread cleanup
1332       // NOTE: Do NOT call socket_shutdown() here - it sends FIN to peer and breaks the connection
1333       // The threads will exit naturally when they check shutting_down/active flags or hit errors
1334       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1335 
1336       // Shutdown packet queues to unblock send thread
1337       if (client->audio_queue) {
1338         packet_queue_stop(client->audio_queue);
1339       }
1340       // Video now uses double buffer, no queue to shutdown
1341 
1342       break;
1343     }
1344   }
1345 
1346   // If client not found, unlock and return
1347   if (!target_client) {
1348     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1349     log_warn("Cannot remove client %s: not found", client_id);
1350     return -1;
1351   }
1352 
1353   // Unregister client from session_host (for discovery mode support)
1354   // NOTE: Client may not be registered if crypto handshake failed before session_host registration
1355   if (server_ctx->session_host && target_client->session_client_id != 0) {
1356     asciichat_error_t session_result =
1357         session_host_remove_client(server_ctx->session_host, target_client->session_client_id);
1358     if (session_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1359       // ERROR_NOT_FOUND (91) is expected if client failed crypto before being registered with session_host
1360       if (session_result == ERROR_NOT_FOUND) {
1361         log_debug("Client %s not found in session_host (likely failed crypto before registration)", client_id);
1362       } else {
1363         log_warn("Failed to unregister client %s from session_host: %s", client_id,
1364                  asciichat_error_string(session_result));
1365       }
1366     } else {
1367       log_debug("Client %s unregistered from session_host", client_id);
1368     }
1369   }
1370 
1371   // Release write lock before joining threads.
1372   // This prevents deadlock with render threads that need read locks.
1373   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1374 
1375   // Phase 2: Stop all client threads
1376   // For TCP clients: use tcp_server thread pool management
1377   // For WebRTC clients: manually join threads (no socket-based thread pool)
1378   // Use is_tcp_client flag, not socket value - socket may already be INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE
1379   // even for TCP clients if it was closed earlier during cleanup.
1380   log_debug("Stopping all threads for client %s (socket %d, is_tcp=%d)", client_id, client_socket,
1381             target_client ? target_client->is_tcp_client : -1);
1382 
1383   if (target_client && target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1384     // TCP client: use tcp_server thread pool
1385     // This joins threads in stop_id order: receive(1), render(2), send(3)
1386     // Use saved client_socket for lookup (tcp_server needs original socket as key)
1387     if (client_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1388       asciichat_error_t stop_result = tcp_server_stop_client_threads(server_ctx->tcp_server, client_socket);
1389       if (stop_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1390         log_warn("Failed to stop threads for TCP client %s: error %d", client_id, stop_result);
1391         // Continue with cleanup even if thread stopping failed
1392       }
1393     } else {
1394       log_debug("TCP client %s socket already closed, threads should have already exited", client_id);
1395     }
1396   } else if (target_client) {
1397     // WebRTC client: manually join threads
1398     log_debug("Stopping WebRTC client %s threads (receive and send)", client_id);
1399 
1400     // Join receive thread (but skip if called from the receive thread itself to avoid deadlock)
1401     thread_id_t current_thread_id = asciichat_thread_self();
1402     if (asciichat_thread_equal(current_thread_id, target_client->receive_thread_id)) {
1403       log_debug("remove_client() called from receive thread for client %s, skipping self-join", client_id);
1404     } else {
1405       void *recv_result = NULL;
1406       asciichat_error_t recv_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->receive_thread, &recv_result);
1407       if (recv_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1408         log_warn("Failed to join receive thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, recv_join_result);
1409       } else {
1410         log_debug("Joined receive thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1411       }
1412     }
1413 
1414     // Join dispatch thread (BEFORE destroying packet queues)
1415     if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->dispatch_thread)) {
1416       atomic_store_bool(&target_client->dispatch_thread_running, false);
1417       void *dispatch_result = NULL;
1418       asciichat_error_t dispatch_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->dispatch_thread, &dispatch_result);
1419       if (dispatch_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1420         log_warn("Failed to join dispatch thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, dispatch_join_result);
1421       } else {
1422         log_debug("Joined dispatch thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1423       }
1424     }
1425 
1426     // Join send thread
1427     void *send_result = NULL;
1428     asciichat_error_t send_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->send_thread, &send_result);
1429     if (send_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1430       log_warn("Failed to join send thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, send_join_result);
1431     } else {
1432       log_debug("Joined send thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1433     }
1434     // Note: Render threads still need to be stopped - they're created the same way for both TCP and WebRTC
1435     // For now, render threads are expected to exit when they check g_should_exit and client->active
1436   }
1437 
1438   // Destroy ACIP transport before closing socket
1439   // For WebSocket clients: LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED already closed and destroyed the transport
1440   // Trying to destroy it again causes heap-use-after-free since LWS callbacks might still fire
1441   // For TCP clients: transport is ours to clean up
1442   if (target_client && target_client->transport && target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1443     acip_transport_destroy(target_client->transport);
1444     target_client->transport = NULL;
1445     log_debug("Destroyed ACIP transport for TCP client %s", client_id);
1446   } else if (target_client && target_client->transport && !target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1447     // WebSocket client - just NULL it out, LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED already destroyed it
1448     target_client->transport = NULL;
1449     log_debug("Skipped transport destruction for WebSocket client %s (LWS already destroyed)", client_id);
1450   }
1451 
1452   // Now safe to close the socket (threads are stopped)
1453   if (client_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1454     log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Closing socket %d for client %s after thread cleanup", client_socket, client_id);
1455     socket_close(client_socket);
1456   }
1457 
1458   // Phase 3: Clean up resources with write lock
1459   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1460 
1461   // Re-validate target_client pointer after reacquiring lock.
1462   // Another thread might have invalidated the pointer while we had the lock released.
1463   if (target_client) {
1464     // Verify client_id still matches and client is still in shutting_down state
1465     bool still_shutting_down = atomic_load_bool(&target_client->shutting_down);
1466     if (strcmp(target_client->client_id, client_id) != 0 || !still_shutting_down) {
1467       log_warn("Client %s pointer invalidated during thread cleanup (id=%s, shutting_down=%d)", client_id,
1468                target_client->client_id, still_shutting_down);
1469       rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1470       return 0; // Another thread completed the cleanup
1471     }
1472   }
1473 
1474   // Mark socket as closed in client structure
1475   if (target_client && target_client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1476     mutex_lock(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1477     target_client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
1478     mutex_unlock(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1479     log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Client %s socket set to INVALID", target_client->client_id);
1480   }
1481 
1482   // Use the dedicated cleanup function to ensure all resources are freed
1483   cleanup_client_all_buffers(target_client);
1484 
1485   // Remove from audio mixer
1486   if (g_audio_mixer) {
1487     mixer_remove_source(g_audio_mixer, client_id);
1488 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
1489     log_debug("Removed client %s from audio mixer", client_id);
1490 #endif
1491   }
1492 
1493   // Remove from uthash table
1494   // Verify client is actually in the hash table before deleting.
1495   // Another thread might have already removed it.
1496   if (target_client) {
1497     client_info_t *hash_entry = NULL;
1498     HASH_FIND_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, target_client->client_id, hash_entry);
1499     if (hash_entry == target_client) {
1500       HASH_DELETE(hh, g_client_manager.clients_by_id, target_client);
1501       log_debug("Removed client %s from uthash table", client_id);
1502     } else {
1503       log_warn("Client %s already removed from hash table by another thread (found=%p, expected=%p)", client_id,
1504                (void *)hash_entry, (void *)target_client);
1505     }
1506   } else {
1507     log_warn("Failed to remove client %s from hash table (client not found)", client_id);
1508   }
1509 
1510   // Cleanup crypto context for this client
1511   if (target_client->crypto_initialized) {
1512     crypto_handshake_destroy(&target_client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
1513     target_client->crypto_initialized = false;
1514     log_debug("Crypto context cleaned up for client %s", client_id);
1515   }
1516 
1517   // Verify all threads have actually exited before resetting client_id.
1518   // Threads that are still starting (at RtlUserThreadStart) haven't checked client_id yet.
1519   // We must ensure threads are fully joined before zeroing the client struct.
1520   // Use exponential backoff for thread termination verification
1521   int retry_count = 0;
1522   const int max_retries = 5;
1523   while (retry_count < max_retries && (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->send_thread) ||
1524                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->receive_thread) ||
1525                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->video_render_thread) ||
1526                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->audio_render_thread))) {
1527     // Exponential backoff: 10ms, 20ms, 40ms, 80ms, 160ms
1528     uint32_t delay_ms = 10 * (1 << retry_count);
1529     log_warn("Client %s: Some threads still appear initialized (attempt %d/%d), waiting %ums", client_id,
1530              retry_count + 1, max_retries, delay_ms);
1531     platform_sleep_us(delay_ms * 1000);
1532     retry_count++;
1533   }
1534 
1535   if (retry_count == max_retries) {
1536     log_error("Client %s: Threads did not terminate after %d retries, proceeding with cleanup anyway", client_id,
1537               max_retries);
1538   }
1539 
1540   // Only reset client_id to 0 AFTER confirming threads are joined
1541   // This prevents threads that are starting from accessing a zeroed client struct
1542   // Reset client_id to NULL before destroying mutexes to prevent race conditions.
1543   // This ensures worker threads can detect shutdown and exit before the mutex is destroyed.
1544   // If we destroy the mutex first, threads might try to access a destroyed mutex.
1545   target_client->client_id[0] = '\0'; // Clear the client_id string
1546 
1547   // Wait for threads to observe the client_id reset
1548   // Use sufficient delay for memory visibility across all CPU cores
1549   platform_sleep_us(5 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 5ms delay for memory barrier propagation
1550 
1551   // Destroy mutexes and condition variables
1552   // IMPORTANT: Always destroy these even if threads didn't join properly
1553   // to prevent issues when the slot is reused
1554   mutex_destroy(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1555   mutex_destroy(&target_client->send_mutex);
1556   cond_destroy(&target_client->dispatch_queue_cond);
1557 
1558   // Clear client structure
1559   // NOTE: After memset, the mutex handles are zeroed but the OS resources
1560   // have been released by the destroy calls above
1561   memset(target_client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
1562 
1563   // Recalculate client count
1564   int remaining_count = 0;
1565   for (int j = 0; j < MAX_CLIENTS; j++) {
1566     if (g_client_manager.clients[j].client_id[0] != '\0') {
1567       remaining_count++;
1568     }
1569   }
1570   g_client_manager.client_count = remaining_count;
1571 
1572   log_debug("Client removed: client_id=%s (%s) removed, remaining clients: %d", client_id, display_name_copy,
1573             remaining_count);
1574 
1575   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1576 
1577   // Broadcast updated state
1578   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1579 
1580   return 0;
1581 }

int
  start_webrtc_client_threads (server_context_t * server_ctx, const char
  * client_id)


Start threads for a WebRTC client after crypto initialization.
    This is called by WebSocket handler after crypto handshake is initialized.
    It ensures receive thread doesn't try to process packets before crypto
    context exists.




Parameters


server_ctx Server context


client_id Client ID to start threads for


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure

2632     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Server context is NULL");
2633     return -1;
2634   }
2635

2637   if (!client) {
2638     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Client %s not found", client_id);
2639     return -1;
2640   }
2641 
2642   log_debug("Starting threads for WebRTC client %s...", client_id);
2643   return start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, false);

void
  stop_client_threads (client_info_t * client)


Definition at line 2646 of file
  src/server/client.c.


2646                                                 {
2647   if (!client) {
2648     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Client is NULL");
2649     return;
2650   }
2651 
2652   // Signal threads to stop
2653   log_debug("Setting active=false in stop_client_threads (client_id=%s)", client->client_id);
2654   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
2655   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
2656 
2657   // Wait for threads to finish
2658   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&client->send_thread)) {
2659     asciichat_thread_join(&client->send_thread, NULL);
2660   }
2661   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&client->receive_thread)) {
2662     asciichat_thread_join(&client->receive_thread, NULL);
2663   }
2664   // For async dispatch: stop dispatch thread if running
2665   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&client->dispatch_thread)) {
2666     atomic_store_bool(&client->dispatch_thread_running, false);
2667     asciichat_thread_join(&client->dispatch_thread, NULL);
2668   }
2669 }

Variable
  Documentation




client_manager_t g_client_manager


Global client manager singleton - central coordination point.
    Global client manager.




This is the primary data structure for managing all connected
    clients. It serves as the bridge between main.c's connection accept loop and
    the per-client threading architecture.




STRUCTURE COMPONENTS:


    
  
  • clients[]: Array backing storage for client_info_t structs
    • 
  
  • client_hashtable: O(1) lookup table for client_id ->
    client_info_t*
    • 
  
  • client_count: Current number of active clients
    • 
  
  • mutex: Legacy mutex (mostly replaced by rwlock)
    • 
  
  • next_client_id: Monotonic counter for unique client identification
    • 



THREAD SAFETY: Protected by g_client_manager_rwlock for concurrent
    access


Definition at line 333 of file
  src/server/client.c.


Referenced by add_client(), add_webrtc_client(),
    any_clients_sending_video(),
    broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(), find_client_by_id(),
    find_client_by_socket(), remove_client(),
    send_server_state_to_client(), server_main(),
    stats_logger_thread(), and update_server_stats().






rwlock_t
  g_client_manager_rwlock = {0}


Reader-writer lock protecting the global client manager.
    Read-write lock protecting client manager.




This lock enables high-performance concurrent access patterns:


    
  
  • Multiple threads can read client data simultaneously (stats,
    rendering)
    • 
  
  • Only one thread can modify client data at a time (add/remove
    operations)
    • 
  
  • Eliminates contention between read-heavy operations
    • 



USAGE PATTERN:


    
  
  • Read operations: rwlock_rdlock() for client lookups, stats gathering
    • 
  
  • Write operations: rwlock_wrlock() for add_client(),
      remove_client()
    • 
  
  • Always acquire THIS lock before per-client mutexes (lock ordering)
    • 



Definition at line 348 of file
  src/server/client.c.


348 {0};

    broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(), find_client_by_id(),
    find_client_by_socket(), remove_client(),

    update_server_stats().
